title: State can't deny maternity benefits to pregnant women citing 80-day work rule: Madhya Pradesh High Court
description: The Madhya Pradesh High Court has ruled that a rule requiring women employees to have worked at least 80 days for being eligible to avail maternity leave benefits does not apply to State-run establishments [Dr Priti Saket v The State of Madhya Pradesh and Others].Section 5(2) of the Maternity Benefit Act states that a woman employed in an establishment for less than 80 days before the date of expected delivery shall not be entitled to the maternity benefits.However, Justice Vishal Dhagat said th
